title: Uncharted Arabian Chicken Roast
description: A fragrant, mildly spiced chicken roast in earthenware, featuring coriander and gentle Ayurvedic-friendly flavors suitable for a royal getaway meal.
instructions: In a small bowl mix coriander powder, cumin, paprika, salt and pepper.
Pat the chicken thighs dry with paper towels.
Brush each thigh with olive oil and rub in the spice mix, then scatter fresh coriander leaves on top.
Place the sliced onion in the bottom of a ttukbaegi earthenware pot and arrange the chicken thighs on top.
Squeeze lemon juice over the chicken and add smashed garlic cloves around the pieces.
Cover the pot with its lid and bake in the preheated oven for 45 minutes, or until the chicken reaches an internal temperature of 75°C (165°F).
Remove the lid, stir gently to mix the juices, and bake uncovered for an additional 10 minutes to crisp the skin.
Let the roast rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve the chicken with a side of warm flatbread and a simple cucumber salad.
Rest of the steps are optional: garnish with extra fresh coriander or a drizzle of yogurt if desired.
